_comment: Generated from ui/src/lib/ontology-data.json for the embedded RIPE-KG WebVOWL viewer.

description.en: Combined RIPE-O and InspectAI ontology view used by the RIPE-KG application.

comment.en: A ripe:Author is a prov:Person and foaf:Person representing a person primarily responsible for a resource represented in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. Such resources may include works and other pieces of information, such as peer comments. The author of a journal article or the person named as responsible for a peer comment are examples of ripe:Author.

A ripe:AutomatedAgent is a prov:SoftwareAgent representing a software agent that participates in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment by gathering evidence, deriving assessment-relevant information, or producing initial outcomes. A software that can perform ripe:EvidenceAggregation and produce ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis instances without human intervention is modeled as a ripe:AutomatedAgent.

A ripe:CorrectionNotice is a fabio:Correction and ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence representing a formal correction notice about a ripe:Work and used as evidence in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. For example, an erratum correcting a published article is a ripe:CorrectionNotice.

A ripe:EvidenceAggregation is a prov:Activity representing the aggregation of evidence and source records used in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. It provides provenance for the evidence collected for one ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. Retrieval of registry records and publication notices for one assessed work is one such activity.

A ripe:ExpressionOfConcern is a fabio:Comment and ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence representing a formal journal or publisher notice that raises concerns about a ripe:Work and is used as evidence in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. For example, a publisher notice questioning the reliability of a trial report is an expression of concern.

A ripe:HumanReviewer is a prov:Person and foaf:Person representing a human agent who participates in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment by evaluating evidence and recording ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis instances. A systematic reviewer investigating research integrity of a study is a ripe:HumanReviewer.

A ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence is a tido:Evidence item used to support, challenge, or contextualise a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. Publication metadata, registry evidence, study-design evidence, publication notices, and peer comments used in an integrity assessment are examples of ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence.

A ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis is a tido:Hypothesis that records an automated or human-reviewed answer to a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentQuestion. For example, a reviewer or automated agent may produce a hypothesis that a study has no concerns, some concerns, or serious concerns.

A ripe:IntegrityAssessmentQuestion is a tido:RQ and prov:Entity representing one specific integrity-related question investigated within a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. It is answered by one or more ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis instances via tido:answers. An example of a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentQuestion is a question asking whether a ripe:Work has an associated retraction.

A ripe:OverallIntegrityAssessmentQuestion is a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentQuestion representing the overall integrity question investigated for a ripe:Work within a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. It is answered by one or more ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis instances that synthesise the component integrity assessment questions and their corresponding hypotheses.

A ripe:PeerComment is a fabio:Comment and ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence representing a post-publication comment from the research community about a ripe:Work and used as evidence in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. For example, a PubPeer comment discussing methodological concerns is a ripe:PeerComment.

A ripe:PublicationDetails is a fabio:BibliographicMetadata and ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence representing bibliographic metadata describing a ripe:Work. It may record values such as title, publication venue, publisher, identifiers, and publication date. A record containing title, journal, publisher, and publication date for a trial article is one such instance.

A ripe:RegistryEvidence is a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence representing information recorded in a trial registry about a ripe:Work. It may record values such as the registry identifier, registration date, registry name, registry link, and the associated registration assessment rationale. A ClinicalTrials.gov or WHO ICTRP entry associated with the assessed work is one such instance.

A ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment represents one integrity assessment of a ripe:Work. It brings together the assessed work, the ripe:IntegrityAssessmentQuestion instances investigated for that work, the ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis instances answering those questions, the ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence instances supporting them, and the activities that generated or reviewed those outcomes. An assessment of a single published clinical trial report is one such instance.

A ripe:RetractionNotice is a fabio:RetractionNotice and ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence representing a formal retraction notice used as evidence in a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. For example, a journal-issued retraction notice for a trial article is a ripe:RetractionNotice.

A ripe:StudyDesignEvidence is a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence representing information about the design and timeline of a study relevant to a ripe:Work. It may record values such as recruitment start, recruitment end, and study end dates, together with rationales supporting those assigned values. A record describing the recruitment and completion timeline of a clinical study is one such instance.

A ripe:Work is a fabio:Work and prov:Entity representing a publication that is directly assessed or otherwise referred to within a ripe:ResearchIntegrityAssessment. It includes the assessed work itself and other related publications, such as cited works or original articles associated with publication notices. For example, a trial article under review is a ripe:Work.
Links a research integrity assessment to the work being assessed.
Links evidence to the work or author that the evidence concerns.
Links evidence to the work or author that the evidence concerns.

Records whether a ripe:RegistryEvidence instance supports the judgement that the study was prospectively registered. For example, a value of true indicates that registration was assessed as preceding the relevant study start point.

Records a textual justification attached to a tido:PieceOfInformation, such as an extracted value or an assessment conclusion. For example, it may store a reviewer note defending a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis.
Records the date value assigned as the participant recruitment end in a ripe:StudyDesignEvidence instance. For example, it may capture an enrolment end date reported in the article text.

Records the textual justification for assigning a value to ripe:recruitmentEndDate in a ripe:StudyDesignEvidence instance. For example, the agent responsible for gathering the ripe:recruitmentEndDate may state that the end of enrolment was inferred from the methods described in the study protocol.

Records the date value assigned as the participant recruitment start in a ripe:StudyDesignEvidence instance. For example, it may capture a month-year or date string when the first participant was enrolled for a study.

Records the textual justification for assigning a value to ripe:recruitmentStartDate in a ripe:StudyDesignEvidence instance. For example, it may note that a sentence describing when enrolment began was interpreted as the recruitment start date by the agent responsible.

Records the textual justification for the registration-status assessment represented by a ripe:RegistryEvidence instance. For example, it may explain that the registry submission date was compared with the recruitment start date.
Records the registration date value associated with a ripe:RegistryEvidence instance. For example, it may store the submission date shown in a ClinicalTrials.gov record.
Records the registry identifier associated with a ripe:RegistryEvidence instance. For example, this may be an NCT or ISRCTN number.
Records the URI of the registry record represented by a ripe:RegistryEvidence instance. For example, it may point to a ClinicalTrials.gov or WHO ICTRP record page.
Records the name of the trial registry represented by a ripe:RegistryEvidence instance. For example, values may include ClinicalTrials.gov, ISRCTN, or WHO ICTRP.

Records the lexical outcome assigned to a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentHypothesis in answer to a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentQuestion. For example, the agents may assign no-concerns, some-concerns, or serious-concerns in response to a question about the study's integrity.

Records the source identifier assigned by the Retraction Watch database to a ripe:IntegrityAssessmentEvidence item when such an identifier is available. In the RIPE-KG, this is used for retraction notices, expressions of concern, and correction notices sourced from Retraction Watch.
Records the date value assigned as the study end in a ripe:StudyDesignEvidence instance. For example, it may capture a reported study completion or final follow-up date.

Records the textual justification for assigning a value to ripe:studyEndDate in a ripe:StudyDesignEvidence instance. For example, it may explain why a final follow-up date was treated as the study end date.
